title: Pine forest canopy modulates shrub–oak seedling interactions depending on shrub functional identity
About
text:
I am a PhD researcher studying how plants interact and how Mediterranean forests regenerate. Through this blog, I share analyses, technical notes, research challenges, and reflections that emerge throughout the scientific process.
It is not knowledge until it is shared.

title: What I’m doing now
text:
Currently undertaking a predoctoral research stay at the University of Bordeaux, where I am refining the analyses for my study on facilitative interactions between shrubs and oaks (Que_facil Project).
Working on my upcoming poster for the 68th Annual Symposium of the International Association for Vegetation Science (IAVS).
Based primarily in Alcalá de Henares, Spain, where I work as a PhD researcher in the Forest Ecology and Restoration Research Group at the University of Alcalá.
Working on a research article about desiccation sensitivity (recalcitrance) in acorns of European oak species.
Always exploring new ways to share science. This blog is part of that exploration.
